cards.grammarNote: 안 = don't/not (choice or state). 못 = can't (physical inability). 안 움직여요 = I'm not moving. 못 움직여요 = I can't move (physically unable).
걷다 (to walk) → 걸어요. ㄷ-irregular: ㄷ → ㄹ before vowel endings. 못 걸어요 = physically unable to walk.
움직이다 (to move) → 움직여요. 못 움직여요 = I can't move. Used to describe physical inability due to pain or injury.
잠 = sleep (noun), 을 = object marker. 자다 (to sleep) → 자요. 잠을 못 자요 = I can't sleep. 안 자요 = I'm not sleeping (choice).
가만히 = still/quietly. 있다 can mean 'to stay'. 못 있어요 = can't stay/be still. Used for restlessness or pain.
듣다 (to hear) → 들어요. ㄷ-irregular. 잘 못 들어요 = can't hear well. 잘 안 들어요 = not hearing well (different nuance — 못 implies inability).
보이다 (to be visible) → 보여요. 잘 안 보여요 = can't see well. 보이다 is a passive form of 보다 — used when the ability to see is affected.
쓰다 (to use/write) → 써요. ㅡ-irregular: drop ㅡ, add -어요. 손을 = hand (with object marker 을). 못 써요 = can't use.
아프다 (to hurt) → 아파요. ㅡ-irregular. 안 아파요 = doesn't hurt. 머리 = head. No subject marker needed in casual Korean.
-아서/-어서 = because. 아파서 = because I'm sick. 못 먹어요 = can't eat. The -서 ending gives the reason.
기운 = energy/vitality. 없어요 = there isn't / I don't have. 기운이 없어요 = I have no energy/I feel drained.
안 되다 → 안 돼요 = it's not working / it won't do. Used when something (including a body function) isn't functioning. 잘 안 돼요 = it's not working well.
